stem: An engineer takes 20 measurements of room temperature using a thermometer. The measurements have a mean of 155.2 °C and a standard deviation of 12.2 °C. However, calibration tests reveal that a thermometer systematically overestimates the true value by between 4.3% and 8.5%. Estimate the true room temperature and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 155.2 °C. After correcting for the 6.4% bias: ≈ 145.9 °C. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.3%-8.5%) and sampling variability: [137.5, 154.3]. Point estimate: 145.9 °C.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
